title: Selectors, Cardinality, Domain Types, and Extraction Drift · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs selectors, cardinality, domain types, and extraction drift from revision, authorization, seed, URL, robots decision, request, response bytes, tree identity, record provenance, frontier transition, goroutine owner, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field.
Return explicit zero, one, many, invalid, conflicting, and fallback outcomes rather than hiding them behind Find and First.
Parse external strings through constructors for URLs, language, dates, identifiers, enumerations, and bounded text.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Track cardinalities, fallback use, rejection rate, representative fixtures, schema revision, and changed markup alerts. Artifact under review: typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Selector Drift, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Selector Drift
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Selector Drift: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 7525 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track cardinalities, fallback use, rejection rate, representative fixtures, schema revision, and changed markup alerts.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Selector Drift.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Selector Drift, keep Selector Drift connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Track cardinalities, fallback use, rejection rate, representative fixtures, schema revision, and changed markup alerts.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Selector Drift, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Selector Drift precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Selector Drift, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 7525 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track cardinalities, fallback use, rejection rate, representative fixtures, schema revision, and changed markup alerts.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Selector Drift, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Broken selectors always panic or return zero matches.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Selector Scope to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Selector Scope.

checks.description: The decision applies Cardinality Result to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Cardinality Result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using track and cardinalities plus specific case evidence.
